page.content: In Table 1, the analysis of tumor types reveals that for squamous cell carcinoma (SCC), there are 172 cases with C44Mab-5 immunostaining showing 14 positive cases at 1+, 20 at 2+, and 61 at 3+, totaling 77 positive cases, which represents 158 out of 172 or 91.9%. In adenoid cystic carcinoma (ACC), out of 7 cases, 2 are positive at 1+, 3 at 2+, and 1 at 3+, resulting in 5 positive cases, translating to 5 out of 7 or 71.4%. For mucoepidermoid carcinoma (MEC), all 3 cases show 0 at 1+, 1 at 2+, and 2 at 3+, leading to 3 positive cases, accounting for 3 out of 3 or 100%.

page.content: The total across all tumor types shows 182 cases, with 16 positive at 1+, 24 at 2+, and 62 at 3+, culminating in 80 positive cases, which means 166 out of 182 or 91.2%. Table 2 presents the change of CAT scores after varying time periods: for YQGB, there are 27 with ≥2 points and 5 with <2 points after one month, 32 with ≥2 points and 1 with <2 points after two months, and 33 with ≥2 points and 0 with <2 points after three months. Pb shows 18 with ≥2 points and 10 with <2 points after one month, 20 with ≥2 points and 4 with <2 points after two months, and 25 with ≥2 points and 6 with <2 points after three months.

page.content: The statistical significance for X2 is 3.214 after one month, 3.229 after two months, and 7.048 after three months, with P values of 0.073 after one month, 0.072 after two months, and 0.01 after three months.
